lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Don't talk to me outerlander! I don't have time for the likes of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! Don't call me outerlander! I'm known as the mighty [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Can't say I've heard of you. So they let your sort into the clan nowadays? Wow. Things have really changed since I've been gone...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look like a Fremennik yourself...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Yes, I suppose that's true... I have been gone such a long time...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been away from these parts a while?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Oh, you haven't heard of the infamous Lokar, scourge of the northern seas?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... no... That's you then, is it?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Forgotten already? Tch! I have no regrets about why I left!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you leave?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: As a young man, I learnt that the Fremennik way of life was too slow for me...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Oh, sure, every so often we got to go on big raiding parties, looting and pillaging the weaker islands around here for food and profit, but...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Well, after a while, that was all I looked forward to doing.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Growing crops, milking cows, catching and cooking food, the day to day activities of our life...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: THEY'RE SO BORING!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: No, I knew what I wanted to do, and that was attack innocent people going about their daily business, steal all their money, then spend it on wine, women and song!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.... Kaaaaay....

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: So I ran away from Rellekka to become a PIRATE! It's way more fun. Plus you get to see the world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was the world good then? Only, you seem to have returned to Rellekka after all.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Yes, I have returned here, but that's only because we're short on supplies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So where are you and your scurvy sea-dogs headed to next? Arrr shiver-me timbers!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: You're trying my patience...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, got caught up in the moment.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Indeed. Well, the rest of the group are at Lunar Isle at the moment. It's where the Moon Clan live.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Moon Clan... I think I've heard of them...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: I'd be surprised if you hadn't, they're the Fremenniks' most hated enemy!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: They've been at war with them for hundreds of years!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Hmmm. Actually, that might be a problem for you then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A problem?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Yeah, they're a really powerful race of mages. They'd read your mind and see that you're a Fremennik straight away, then probably turn you inside out and feed you to the birds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cosmic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you went there okay?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Yeah, but I think the Fremenniks are a bunch of losers. They probably picked up on that.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Look, I know a way you'll be able to travel there without, you know, getting killed and that, if you fancy a trip!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not, I've always wondered what the state of my innards are!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: That's the spirit!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Well, what you want to do is go and talk to Brundt the Chieftain and get a Seal of Passage from him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Seal of Passage? What's that?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Well, it shows that you're a diplomat for the Fremennik, so you'll have immunity from being killed and stuff.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: It's an ancient tradition not to attack anyone bearing a Seal of Passage, because they're probably on a diplomatic mission. Or well connected with powerful allies. Either way, killing them is usually a bad idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I have to try and bring peace to two warring nations who have been fighting for centuries?!?!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: What? I never said you had to bother trying to do that, you just have to pretend like you might.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: No skin off my nose if those losers want to get slaughtered by the Moon Clan, just more stuff for me to loot when they're all dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I will try. I am the mighty [Fremennik name] after all!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Yes....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just have to go talk to Brundt the Chieftain and tell him I want a Seal of Passage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it? Because in my experience simple tasks like that always seem to take longer than I expect...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Yeah, just go over and talk to the chief loser. You might have to tell him some rubbish he wants to hear, but that's it.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Come back here when you got it, my supplies are loaded and I'm good to head back to our ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I'd like to live.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Oh, so you're a loser like the rest of the Fremenniks then? See ya around, loser.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, 'bye then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, 'bye then.

sections.title: Talking to Lokar Searunner before getting the seal of passage

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Hey there [Fremennik name]. So you get that seal thingy off of the Brundt the Chieftain yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... Not yet...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Well hurry it up, okay? I'm sick of this dump of a town, I want to go back to the ship!

sections.title: Before getting the seal of passage

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there Brundt the Chieftain. Could you help me out with a Seal of Passage?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: A Seal of Passage? Whatever for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've heard that there have been great troubles between your people and that of the Moon Clan. I was hoping to help. And so I need the Seal of Passage to show my intentions.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah [Fremennik name], many have tried, but The Moon Clan are by no means a sharing group of people. They do everything by magic, something we never use. But they are so secretive about it. If only they would share, we

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: wouldn't have to kill them all the time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have that seal then?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes, but you have no room to carry it. You should have thought of that before, [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you are to bring peace to our two warring clans, then perhaps it is best that you do...

sections.title: After losing the seal of passage

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost my Seal of Passage!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So you want another one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I would if I had space to carry one.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Oh dear...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Then you had better take a replacement and be less careless in future.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah, [Fremennik name]. How goes the peace talks with the accursed Moon Clan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you know. Ongoing negotiations.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I see. Well, I don't hold out for much hope!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you're confident in my abilities.

sections.title: After losing the seal of passage

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: So you got that seal yet from Brundt the Chieftain? Can I leave this dump of a town?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah... Well, Brundt the Chieftain gave me a seal, but I think I lost it somewhere along the way...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: What are you? Useless? Go get another one from Brundt the Chieftain, and hurry it up, I don't want to be here any longer than I have to be!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: So you got that seal yet from Brundt the Chieftain? Can I leave this dump of a town?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, I've got it right here!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Great! Then let's head back to my ship! You ready to go now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrr! Yar! Let's be on our way, yar!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: ...Why are you talking like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... isn't that how pirates talk?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Only the very stupid ones...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: I suggest you DON'T talk like that to my crew. They will probably assume you are mocking them and kill you.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: So let's start again: Are you ready to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why yes! Tally-ho!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: I suppose that will have to do.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Here we are at the Pirates' Cove! You'll find the captain already on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just yet...

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Well hurry it up okay? This place is a dump, and I'm a vicious blood-thirsty pirate, not your personal travel agent.

sections.title: Talking to Lokar Searunner at Pirates' Cove

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Lokar.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Hi again [Fremennik name]! What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I still need the Seal of Passage?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Yeah, why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost it.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Silly you. You'll have to go back to Brundt the Chieftain to get another before you can go on to Lunar Isle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me back to Rellekka please?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Hey, if you want to go back to loserville with all the losers, who am I to stop you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing thanks! I just saw you here and thought I'd say hello!

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Hey, I knew you seemed cool when I met you [Fremennik name]!

sections.title: Talking to Lokar Searunner in Rellekka after visiting Pirates' Cove

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: You won't make many friends on Lunar Isle without your Seal of Passage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's fine, I'm just going to Pirates' Cove.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'd better go back and get it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lokar, can you take me back to your ship?

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: Sheesh, make your mind pal, I'm not a taxi service!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind. Again. I don't want to go.

lines.speaker: Lokar Searunner
lines.text: You are possibly the most indecisive person I have ever met...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, 'bye then.

sections.title: Talking to the crew before setting sail

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Afternoon.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Hey, how you doing? You a stowaway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (sigh) NO, I am NOT a stowaway...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And to be honest, I don't know why everybody seems to think I am...

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Hey, I'm not here to judge you, if you're a stowaway you picked a good ship for it!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: There's lots of convenient hiding places around here - I should know, whenever I'm in trouble with the Captain I find myself a good barrel to hide in, until the fuss dies down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I've always wondered...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly is it that a 'cabin boy' actually does on a pirate ship anyway?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Mostly make coffee. Occasionally I have to swab the decks, but pirates aren't exactly known for cleanliness, you know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. See anything good through your telescope there?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Not really, just those creatures on the island. Don't worry, we sent some men out there to deal with them.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: The captain said we might have a barbecue out there later...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem unhappy about that... Wouldn't a barbecue on the beach be fun?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: You obviously haven't met the cook yet...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, how you doing?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: It is customary when stowing away on a vessel to not introduce yourself to the Captains First Mate, oh foolish one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! I'm not a stowaway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That Lokar guy invited me aboard...

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I see. Well, don't distract me as I'm making preparations for departure.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Try not to distract any of the crew either, Zamorak knows it's hard enough to get them to do any work around here without strangers wandering round the ship asking them inane questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... What are you doing?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Me? I'm the navigator. My job is to ensure the ship travels where it's supposed to go once it's moving by constantly checking our

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: bearings against our plotted course, using charts and a sextant.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: People often think that we just sail off when we feel like it, but its actually far more complicated than that!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Why, when you think of just how much sea there is out there, ANYTHING could happen if there wasn't a competent navigator on board ensuring that we reach our destination safely and in the shortest possible time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. So... What are you doing?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: As I say, being a navigator is a real skill - you really have to think on your feet!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yesiree, once we get sailing, it's all up to me to make sure we don't run into trouble or go off course!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yep, as the rest of the crew basically stand around not doing much, I'll be the one in the cabin, comparing wind speeds, landmarks and charts with my sextant, making sure that we get to the destination on time, and on

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: course!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: You could almost say that I'm the linchpin of the entire operation! Why, if it wasn't for me, the rest of the guys might be sailing for, oooh, hours, and be slightly off course and

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: never even known it!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: They might even be a few hours late for their destination! Or a few miles down the road from it!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: And then where would they be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A few hours late or a few miles down the road?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Exactly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. So... What are you doing?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, just relaxing while I wait for the ship to cast off.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: When we're sailing I'll be very busy you know! I'm probably the single most important person on board when we're sailing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, OKAY!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: The galley ain't open fer food yet whipper-snapper, ye'll hafta come back later if'n ye be wanting food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... something something open, something something food?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye, ter be sure, yer yella-bellied land-lizard!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

sections.title: Talking to Captain Bentley before setting sail

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aye-aye cap'n!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Hmmmm? I don't recognise you as one of my men, what are you doing aboard my ship?

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Stowaway! Stowaway! *Squark*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, Lokar offered me a journey. He said you could take me to the island of the Moon Clan.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Oh he DID, did he? (sigh) I sometimes regret ever allowing that vagabond to join the crew.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: He has a very bad work ethic, and I have to say his attitude can be very confrontational.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Lokar's a loser! *Brakawk*!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... You won't take me to Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well now, I didn't say that.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: We are returning there again soon anyway, as our last visit was most profitable.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: In fact as soon as our boys are finished loading stocks we should be ready to depart!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Now, as captain of this ship I feel I must play host, even if you aren't exactly a welcome guest, so was there anything you wished to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... What can you tell me about this 'Moon Clan'? It all seems very mysterious I must say...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: The Moon Clan? Oh, very fine people. Very hospitable.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: We had heard that they are very wary of strangers, and dislike people just showing up on their island, but when we sent a landing party aboard they all seemed more than happy to see us.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, they even threw a big festival in our honour!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Anyway, they had a large supply of items that we felt we could use on the high seas, so we made a few trades with them, and were on our way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kinds of items?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, I'm not sure how much you've heard about them, but they are magical experts.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: They sold us many rune-stones, as well as a few good luck charms and talismans and such to help us on our voyage.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes, they might have been a little secretive about that strange temple, and the old lodge of theirs in town, and I must say they certainly have the strangest house I have ever seen in my life in their little village, but it was

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: a pleasant trip all round, and I look forward to trading there again in the future.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: After all, it's not everywhere that a pirate captain and his crew can rest ashore without having to worry about the authorities catching them, is it?

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Fight the law! *Squawk*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I guess it isn't.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Was there anything else you wished to ask me about while I'm not busy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were you doing at Lunar Isle anyway? I always thought pirates would be... You know. Out pirating.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, obviously the actual pirating is a big part of our agenda, but we like to think of ourselves as non- regulated merchants.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: What good is plunder if you have nothing to spend it on, hmmmm?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Likewise, I am a forward thinking captain, and endeavour to invest much of my plunder back into the ship and its crew, so that we can be a better and more efficient force for theft upon the seas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you went to Lunar Isle for supplies?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, obviously not initially. Obviously, our first thought was to show up, rob them blind, then take off again with the plunder spilling from our decks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what made you change your mind?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, firstly there were the monsters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: M-monsters?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Oh yes, hundreds of the things. The island's swarming with them you know.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Dirty smelly beasts, as big as a wolf! Yes, you'd certainly better be either handy with a blade or quick on your toes if you wanted to spend much time outside of their village with those things around

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: everywhere.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Squawk* Run fer yer lives lads, it's a monster! *Squawk*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you didn't attack the Moon Clan because you were worried you would yourself be attacked by these monsters trying to make your escape?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Oh yes, absolutely. That and the next reason.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The next reason?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes, the next reason being that the Moon Clan are quite stupendously powerful magicians.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: You'll notice it as soon as you look at them, really quite, quite powerful indeed.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: As soon as we saw them we lost any thoughts about trying to attack or steal from them.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Which as it turned out was lucky for us.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Bunch of blooming loonies! *Brakawk*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Oh yes. They can read minds, you see. If we'd shown up there thinking about robbing them they would have just killed us all on the spot.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I did mention that they are extraordinarily powerful magicians, didn't I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes you did.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Ah, I'm glad I did. Because they really, really are. And mind-readers.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes, all in all I'm glad we didn't try any funny business in the end after all.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Plus there's that other good reason.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That reason being?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, when you are a crew of successful pirates, it becomes more and more difficult to ever moor somewhere and spend your loot.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: People are far too quick to call the guards on a troublemaker nowadays.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: So finding a village where people are more than happy to trade with you regardless of who you may have just stolen from and killed.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I like that non-judgemental attitude of theirs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Anything else you wanted to know before we set sail?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping you could tell me a little bit about your ship. I must say, it's really very impressive.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Ah, a connoisseur! Yes, she's named the Lady Zay, and may I say, is one of the finest ships to ever sail the seas!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: It was a steal from the Karamjan shipyards.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: And when I say it was a steal, I mean to say that myself and some of the lads broke in and stole it one night.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I don't know who they were building these warships for, but whoever it was seemed to be a little on the short side.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: We had to do a little remodelling of the doors and rooms so that we didn't have to crouch around everywhere, but I've finally got her just how I want her, and we have enough cannons down below decks to

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: bombard a small town I'd wager!

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Brawwwwwwk* I want guns, lots of guns! I want guns, lots of guns!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I used to own my own ship you know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was called the Lady Lumbridge if I remember correctly...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder whatever happened to it...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Interesting. You never struck me as being Captain material, but I guess there's a ship out there for everyone...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: The Lady Zay is certainly a big step up from my last ship anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your last ship? Why, what was wrong with it?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, it just wasn't sturdy enough!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I was on a perfectly routine raiding expedition to the east of here, when we ran into a squall, that pushed us towards the rocky shore.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: A few select men and myself managed to escape with our lives intact on a small row boat, but the rest of my crew were washed ashore as the ship was wrecked on those jagged rocks.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Ah yes, it was a tragedy...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: To lose such a fine ship in such an insulting manner...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And of course the rest of your crew...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Who? Oh. Yes. The crew. Yes, it was a tragedy.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I'd paid them in advance you know, I'll never see that money again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... no, I actually meant it was a shame that so many of your crew must have died in the shipwreck.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Was it? Well, if you say so.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Actually, the last I had heard quite a few of them had survived, and had set up a little hideaway next to the remains of the shipwreck.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: If you're particularly worried about them, perhaps you might like to go and pay them a visit someday, and make sure to bring a lot of easily transferable wealth with you.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: They love people visiting with easily transferable wealth.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Now, was there something you wished to talk to me about? Time is pressing on, and we really need to set sail soon if we want to make the most of the winds.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Squawk* Move it or lose it, move it or lose it.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, I would be more than happy to take you, unfortunately the Moon Clan are, frankly, very scary people, and I believe they would not take kindly to us bringing a Fremennik there.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: They are at war with them after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I thought Lokar had already been there?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes. Yes he has. I'm talking about you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Oh yeah, I'm a Fremennik aren't I?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes, yes you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I probably should have kept that Seal Of Passage Brundt the Chieftain gave me with me, shouldn't I?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes, yes you should have. Honestly, you're going to need it with you.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't let you travel with us without it.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Brakawk* Don't drop quest items half way through a quest! *Squawk*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll go and get it again then.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: You do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we sail to Lunar Isle now? I must say, it all sounds very intriguing over there...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: As long as you keep your Seal Of Passage with you at all times while you are on the island you should be okay, even if you are a Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I'm not kidding, they are a very dangerous people, and it would NOT be wise for you to travel there without your Seal Of Passage marking you as a diplomat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, keep my Seal with me at all times, check. Can we go now?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Ah, yes indeed! The winds seem good, so let's make a move!

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: She's blowing a storm, cap'n! *Brarrrrrk*

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well... THAT was deuced strange!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Way to sail a boat?

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Squawk* Way to sail a boat! Way to sail a boat! *Brarrrk*

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: This... This is VERY strange!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, actually, I don't really want to know anything that you can tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In fact, I don't even know why I began talking to you in the first place!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Hmmmm. You could perhaps be more grateful to someone offering you free transport, but then I suppose you are a friend of Lokar's, and a Fremennik, so I suppose the

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: rudeness is natural.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Make sure you are ready for departure, I will be issuing orders to set sail shortly.

sections.title: Talking to the crew after sailing in a circle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the ship?

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: I have no idea! It doesn't normally do that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Weird.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Yup.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the Captain's a very bad sailor I take it?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: No... Usually he's one of the best around, I don't know why we didn't reach our destination like we were supposed to, and it CERTAINLY wasn't anything to do with me

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: or anything I have done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, calm down I wasn't accusing you of anything!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Good, because it has NOTHING to do with me!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: IT'S NOT MY FAULT!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you should lay off the coffee for a bit, youngster...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you reckon it's the navigator or the Captain's fault?

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Yeah, stands to reason, dunnit?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what's wrong with the ship? I've never seen a ship sail in a big circle like that before...

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: I can't say for sure, but it must be either the Captain or the Navigator to blame.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: They're the only ones who control the actual sailing of the ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... Thank you, that's very helpful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you just see that?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: See what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The ship sailing. Or not sailing. Or sailing in a big circle and coming back here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, I'm not a sailor! But whatever it was, it was very odd!

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Can't say that I did, no. Sorry, I was keeping an eye out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An eye out? For what?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Um... Sorry, I can't really say, but I'll know it when I see it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was the ship supposed to go in a big loop like that?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Pretty sure it wasn't, no...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea what caused it?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Absolutely none at all...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty crazy sailing you guys got here!

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Weird, the ships never done that before...

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: In all my years as a pirate, I have never seen such sailing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think caused it?

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: If I was a less superstitious person, I would suggest an evil magic of some kind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Iiiinteresting...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. Pretty rubbish sailing just then, huh?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I have literally never seen anything like it! The ship seemed to... I don't know, suddenly swing round as we approached shore!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: then somehow we were right back where we started! This is all very peculiar if you ask me, I have never seen anything like it in all of my years on the high seas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think might be happening?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Well, my first thought would be that we have jinx aboard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A jinx?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Yes, a jinx. Someone who has recently come aboard and seems to be bringing us bad luck. If it is a jinx, the only answer would be to throw them

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: overboard as shark food.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: You just recently came aboard the ship yourself didn't you? Because I don't recall us having any trouble before you showed up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yes... But... I'm pretty sure I didn't do anything. I hope.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Well, you had better hope it isn't your fault. If there's one thing I hate more than a stowaway, it's a jinx.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: What do you want, spoken to the captain yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but I will.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am, watch, here I go.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, I'm sooooo scared now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, you better be.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: But you're still here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah but not for long.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well you better go then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I'm gone.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: How... bizarre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: You didn't see what the ship just did?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... But I'm not a sailor. Is that abnormal then?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Yes, yes it is. VERY abnormal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Sorry, don't have time to chat! Something's up with the ship, I'm trying to find out what!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Woah! Did you see that crazy trip?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, what do you think happened?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: I dunno, but I'm keeping out of it. I don't want anyone blaming me for it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's driving this thing anyway?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: It's called sailing, but I don't know what's happening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is someone gonna fix it then?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: I hope so. I wanted to pick up some new clothes on Lunar Isle next time we went back.

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: What's happening with the ship???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping you could tell me...

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: Sorry, no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty crazy sailing we did just then, huh?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Did we? Ah, sorry ter say but I missed the whole thing, I've bin a mite busy down here in the ole galley.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We seemed to be getting somewhere, but we're right back where we started!

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye, I've felt like that me whole life ter be honest...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Okay. I'll just leave you to... Whatever it is that you do.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye, that'll be fer the best whipper-snapper, if'n ye be wanting any food cooked at all.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Bentley after sailing in a circle

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I... I don't understand... This is all very strange...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was possibly the most pointless journey I have ever taken in my life.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: The course was plotted, we had a fair tailwind the whole journey...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: BUT WE HAVEN'T BUDGED AN INCH!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I just don't understand it... Maybe you should speak with Jack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Our navigator. He's at the rear of the ship, one floor below deck. He's an old sea-dog with a brown pirate's hat.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Below deck, below deck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we try again?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes, you're right, perhaps it was just a freak wind. Off we go!

sections.title: Talking to 'Birds-Eye' Jack at the Captain's suggestion

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: What do you want, spoken to the captain yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but I will.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am, watch, here I go.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, I'm sooooo scared now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, you better be.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: But you're still here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah but not for long.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well you better go then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I'm gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, are you the navigator?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: That I am. Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Think you can navigate us a course on this ship that isn't a BIG STUPID CIRCLE?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I'm SURE I don't know what you mean!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: C'mon now... If you're the navigator, then it stands to reason it's your fault we didn't sail anywhere!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Now listen here bucko, my course was true, like all of my others, and if you have a problem with how I do my job, then you'd better go and speak to the Captain about it, because I don't appreciate a stowaway

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: wandering in here and criticising me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe I will go and talk to the Captain about you.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: You'd better do that then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I will.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am, watch, here I go.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, I'm sooooo scared now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, you better be.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: But you're still here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah but not for long.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well you better go then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I'm gone.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Bentley after speaking with 'Birds-Eye' Jack

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there captain.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: What are you wanting now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps the navigator made a mistake when he plotted the course?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Hmmm, you think so? Perhaps I'd better ask him about that...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: But... His course looked true.. I just don't understand...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go ask him what happened for you, Captain.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: You will? Well, thank you!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: You're very helpful for a stowaway! He's one floor below deck. To the rear of the ship.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Stowaway! Stowaway! *Squark*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'M NOT A STOWAWAY! GAH!

sections.title: Talking to the crew after suggesting it's the navigator's fault

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard a rumour that the ship isn't going anywhere because of the navigator...

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: I don't deal with gossips.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you think? We keep going round in circles because the navigator is useless?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Yeah, the navigator, absolutely, sounds about right, yup.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: IT'S NOT MY FAULT!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Nope, it has certainly nothing to do with me or anything that I have done to the ship, I swear on my dead mothers life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I spoke to both the Captain and the Navigator, and it seems to me it must be the navigator's fault that we're sailing in circles.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Seems a logical conclusion to me too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glad you think so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. So... That navigator.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Rumours say that it's his fault the ships going round in circles now.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Really? Seems strange to me, I made a point of watching our course last time we sailed, and we didn't deviate at all, but somehow ended up back here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the Captain thinks it must be his fault!

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: No... I'm not so sure about that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This crazy sailing... Think it could be the navigator's fault?

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Well, I guess it could be... Seems a little strange for him to do that though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sailing was horrible. I blame the navigator.

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: You know, speaking ill of someone behind their back isn't cool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, you're right, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The captain seems to think that the navigator is repsonsible for the problems with the ship...

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: He's an arrogant fool, but he gets the job done.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: I'd have to say the Captains wrong about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: So what did the navigator have to say for himself?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Does he have any explanation for why we keep sailing in a big circle every time we try to get to Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, uh, kind of didn't bother to go and talk to him yet...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Squaaaawk* Lazy stowaway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe we were just unlucky that last time?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Oh, you want me to try and set sail again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It can't hurt, right?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I suppose so. Off we go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... The rumour is that it's all the navigator's fault that the ship keeps going around in a big circle.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Is it now? That sounds like exactly the kind of thing that a stowaway and a jinx might say to throw suspicion off of himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think this is my fault?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Ah, so you finally admit you are a stowaway and a jinx!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? NO! Look, I'm pretty sure it's nothing to do with me...

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Well, for your sake I hope you're right. Killing people is such a chore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and speak to the navigator immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bet it's the navigator's fault.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: What is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The weirdness with the ship.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Hmm. Could be I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I blame the navigator for the ships weirdness.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: His fault, eh? Well, I never liked him much anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, me neither.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, think the problems with the ship is the navigator's fault?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Yup, could be. Could well be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What can you tell me about the navigator of this ship?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Sorry, I've never spoken to the man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you never have spoken to him? There's only like 10 people aboard the entire ship!

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Dunno. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know of any reason why the navigator would be deliberately sabotaging our journey?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: No, and I can't believe he would.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So. Absolutely rubbish sailing. It's the navigator's fault, right? You're with me on that, yeah?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye, if'n yer say it's true then it must be!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...and [player name] is the greatest pirate who ever lived?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: [player name] yer say? Aye, well I guess that they must be if'n that's what ye're telling me there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know what? I like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Although admittedly I haven't tried any of your food...

sections.title: Talking to 'Birds-Eye' Jack after suggesting it's the navigator's fault

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... Call yourself a navigator huh? Way to plot a course that's a big circle!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Frankly sirrah your rudeness is quite appalling!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: My calculations and plotted course were expertly calculated, and there can be only one reason why the ship has failed to arrive at its expected destination.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? What's that then? Wrong kind of waves? Your steering wheel got stuck?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I do not have to take this kind of abuse from a common stowaway!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have you know that I am a personal friend of the Captain, and he himself invited me aboard for a small cruise around Lunar Isle along with a variety of local delicacies and beverages!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Really? You don't seem like the type he would make friends with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, absolutely. Me and him go way back. Waaaaaaay back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're like... apples and custard. Everybody says so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, me and Captain Renney, we're a team.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: You mean Captain Bentley?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Bentley. That's what I said.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: No you didn't. You called him Captain Renney.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, yeah, it's like a nickname. Only his friends are allowed to call him that.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yet I have known him for over 20 years and he has never asked to be referred to as anything except Captain Bentley.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, he must hate you.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: And indeed, he has never mentioned any stowaway looking vagabonds as friends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's all besides the point. It's all your fault and I'm going to make sure the Captain fizzles your gizzard, or whatever it is you piratey types do to each other.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: But it wasn't my fault!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Look, see for yourself! All co-ordinates for the journey are carefully plotted to all visible waypoints and landmasses!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: There can only be one POSSIBLE explanation why the ship hasn't reached its destination, as I mentioned before!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? And what would that be then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And your explanation had better be good, or Captain Renney is going to shiver your timbers for good!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: You mean Captain Bentley?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Whatever. Carry on.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Magic. It must be magic. Somebody must have jinxed our ship, but I have no idea who would have done that or why.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Although your highly suspicious behaviour towards me, and mysterious sudden appearance on board at the *exact time* that this jinx appears to have been brought upon us would seem to make you the obvious candidate.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: And we all know what we do to someone who is a jinx.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... We do?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, I'm sure your good friend Captain Bentley has told you all about the last jinx we had on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, absolutely he did, yes. I just uh, have these lapses in memory occasionally.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, it was horrible. I never knew the human body could contain so many mackerel.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: And then that thing they did with the tinderbox and the cheese... I still wake up with nightmares about that sometimes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Right. I'd better go find that jinx then before someone else accuses me of this being my fault.

sections.title: Talking to the crew after learning about the jinx

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it on good authority that there's a jinx on board the ship stopping it sailing anywhere.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: A jinx, you say? My my my, it's been a long time since I've heard of one of those...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about it.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: I'm afraid not, but I hope you find it. They're evil things, especially on a boat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there... Mr. Boy?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Just call me 'boy'. Everybody else does.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you have a real name?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Not that I can remember, no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Right then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, I believe that there may be a jinx aboard this ship, and that is what is preventing us from sailing to Lunar Isle.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: A jinx? I don't know ANYTHING about that!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: IT'S NOT MY FAULT OKAY?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I was actually accusing you of anything there...um, boy.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: You weren't? Oh. IT WAS STILL NOTHING TO DO WITH ME!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're very defensive, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Sorry. It's my job, you know?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I'm always getting the blame for things that go wrong aboard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess you don't know anything useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now, I'm not much of a seafaring guy, but as I understand it this ship has somehow gotten a jinx, which is messing up the sailing to Lunar Isle!

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Really? Oh, that's terrible news.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: I hope someone's doing something about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, yeah, I am.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Good luck with that then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems like the problems with the ship are all down to a jinx.

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Aw no... I hate those things...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The captain thinks there might be a jinx aboard. Any help?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Well, that makes sense, but sorry I don't know anything about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have reason to believe that there is a jinx aboard this ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: IT MIGHT EVEN BE YOU!

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Do I look like a jinx to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... I don't know. I don't know what a jinx looks like.

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: It might be an idea for you to find out before accusing people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh captain, my captain.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: So you've spoken to my navigator again, yes? What does he have to say for himself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes. He tried to hide his incompetence by blaming the ships' problems on a jinx of some sort.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: A JINX you say???

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Aye, I have to admit that does sound like a logical explanation...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm still pretty sure it's just the navigator being really rubbish at his job...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: No, no, a jinx, that makes all too much sense I'm afraid...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, the solution is obvious.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: We need to find who or what has been jinxed and remove it from the ship immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. And when you say 'we' need to find you actually mean...?

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *brakawk* He means you dummy! You dummy! *Squawk*

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, as Captain I have certain responsibilities that must be fulfilled, so I would greatly appreciate a little assistance in tracking down this jinx...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I was afraid you were going to say that...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll try and track down this jinx for you.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Good lad! Let me know if you find out anything important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (sigh) Yes, Captain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey hey hey! Apparently there's a jinx on board. Or the navigator is rubbish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One of the two, I'm not sure which.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Yes, and I believe I'm looking right at him. Would this jinx also be a stowaway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, this calling me a stowaway and a jinx isn't funny!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I quite agree. Perhaps you should leave?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to find who or what this jinx is, if only so I can prove to you that it's not me!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Hmmm. Well, you had best do that then, and stop disturbing me.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: You had better get some evidence quickly as well, I would so hate to have to kill you. Killing someone really tends to ruin my day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: SO! I've found you, YOU JINX!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: What? I'm not a jinx, we've already had this discussion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry. I thought you might be, and that I could trick it out of you by shouting it at you as an accusation.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: That approach works, does it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, not yet, no. But there's always a first time for everything.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I think a more subtle approach might yield you better results.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (sigh) I suppose so... It just won't be as much fun...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard a rumour there's a jinx aboard...

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Really? I hope not, they're evil things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't know anything about a jinx onboard do you?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Nope, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I'm looking for a jinx...

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Why would you be after one of those evil things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think there might be one onboard.

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Gosh, I hope not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. Know anything about jinxes?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Absolutely nothing. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about jinxes?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: Only that they're extremely bad things to have aboard a ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, I'd already figured that much out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I have reason to believe that someone or something has somehow placed a jinx upon this ship, preventing it from sailing as it is supposed to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know anything about that?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: A jinx yer say? Wassat, some kinda fancy foreign vegetable?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... No, no I don't think this ship has been cursed by a fancy foreign vegetable...

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: All vegetables are evil if you ask me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I did ask you...

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: That's right then, we'll hear no more about evil foreign vegetables.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Or evil domestic vegetables.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Or vegetables at all! Death ter vegetables!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then... 'bye.

sections.title: Talking to 'Eagle-eye' Shultz after learning about the jinx

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. So, I'm wondering if you know anything about a jinx at all?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: A jinx you say? Argh, those are evil things!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quick question: What exactly is a jinx anyway?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Ah, well they take many forms.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Sometimes it will just be a person with such staggeringly bad luck that everything they do or say invariably leads them into trouble and commotion, through no fault of their own.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Everything they touch will always bring them misery, and everything they think they have achieved will always end in disaster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: But then there is the other kind of jinx, the even worse kind.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: It is a curse put upon someone, often attached to an object of some kind, and it is done deliberately, so that misfortune will always follow the owner of that object.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why is it such a big deal to sailors?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Ah, well now. When you're on land, bad luck can be an annoyance, and possibly even bring you grief, but it is rarely deadly.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: A jinx on land may fall over tree roots, or lose their coins down a drain, or some other irritation.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: But at sea! With nothing saving you from the cold murky death of drowning except the wood below your feet?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Why, jinxes can kill not just you, but the entire crew of a ship! They are an evil thing indeed, and the only thing you can do to save yourself and your crew is to forcibly

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: remove the jinx from the ship as soon as possible. Dead or alive.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Or casting some kind of anti-curse magic upon the jinxed item might work, but to be honest, throwing them overboard is quicker.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, so someone with magical powers can deliberately place a jinx upon someone or something?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Oh, yes. But they would have to be a very magical and very evil person to risk the lives of an entire ship over some trivial matter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Someone highly magical like, oh, let's say for the sake of argument, the inhabitants of Lunar Isle, a.k.a the Moon Clan?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Well, I suppose so... But for them to curse our entire boat?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Somebody from the landing party must have done something highly offensive to them at the feast for them to place such an evil enchantment that risks the lives of the entire crew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So somebody from the landing party must have gotten themselves jinxed...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks a lot 'Eagle-eye', you've been extremely helpful.

sections.title: Talking to the crew while in search of the jinx

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. Now... about this jinx...

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have reason to believe that it may have been caused by somebody who has offended the Moon Clan somehow while on that recent visit of yours to Lunar Isle.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup. I'm afraid so.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: That doesn't sound like anyone on board...

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: We may be pirates, but we're all surprisingly well- mannered!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't know anything about it?

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Hey. What's up?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's like this: Apparently one of the crew was up to something on Lunar Isle that caused the Moon Clan to be offended, which has led to this jinx being placed on someone or

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: something aboard the ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the closest I can figure it anyway, I have to admit this is all very confusing...

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I SWEAR I DIDN'T MEAN TO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...What?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: ...Nothing. I didn't do anything. At all.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: And nobody can prove that I did, even if I had, which I didn't so they can't. So there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, are you telling me you might have had something to do with this?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: No, I'm telling you the EXACT OPPOSITE!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: It wasn't me! I SWEAR!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? Because a minute ago it really sounded to me as though you-

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: NO! NOT ME! NUH-UH! NO WAY!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, I believe you, I believe you. There's no need to shout at me.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Sorry, I just didn't want you getting the wrong impression and telling the Captain anything, is all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough, I can understand that. Well, see you around.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: IT WASN'T -

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Oh, I mean, goodbye stowaway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Hi, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering...

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel kind of awkward asking...

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Ah, just come out and say it. I'm a pirate, it's not like I get offended easily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually it's about offending people. You didn't, you know, tick off any of the Moon Clan while you were there recently did you?

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Gosh, I hope not. They might have been very hospitable towards us, but there was definitely something spooky about them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I suppose you didn't see anyone acting untoward around them?

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Sorry, can't say as I did...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Hey, no problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... Any ideas who might have offended the Moon Clan and caused them to place a jinx on the ship?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: No, I'm afraid I have no idea. I guess it must have happened when we were all on shore leave, but I honestly don't remember much about it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. No ideas at all?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyone from the crew you think would be particularly offensive to a bunch of highly powerful magic users?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Well, we're something of a mixed bag around here as I'm sure you've noticed, but...

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Sorry. Nobody springs to mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Hiya.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. I don't suppose you have any juicy gossip about that trip to Lunar Isle do you?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Gossip? Like what?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: I heard a really funny story about the First Mate, the captain's parrot and a coconut!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... I was thinking more something about someone behaving very badly and offending the Moon Clan somehow...

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Sorry, I wouldn't know anything about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. Erm, don't take this the wrong way or anything, but are you particularly offensive?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: What?!?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does your mere presence at any occasion cause young children to start crying, old ladies to faint, that kind of thing?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: I'm not sure I like your tone of voice!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, all I want to know is if your terrible disregard for social niceties has caused a jinx to be levelled upon this ship and all who sail in her.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: ABSOLUTELY NOT!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks! All I wanted to know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for somebody who may have offended the Moon Clan on your recent visit to Lunar Isle...

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: I'm sure I know nothing about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. I was hoping you did.

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [player name] on the bridge, Captain!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: What? Oh, it's you.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: You have discovered something about this 'jinx' then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... possibly. Although I'm convinced this is all the navigator's fault. I just don't trust that guy!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yes, you have made your feelings towards the navigator abundantly clear.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Now what have you discovered about this jinx that has been placed upon my ship?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I reckon that somebody from the landing party that went to Lunar Isle did something to offend the Moon Clan, and as retaliation they have placed a jinx upon the

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ship.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I see. Well, that seems to make sense.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: So who was it who has caused this, and how can I lift the jinx?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... well... I, uh, didn't find anything out about that yet.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Squaaaawk* Lazy stowaway!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I see. Well, I suggest you ask the crew if they know anything, and let me know the second you have a solution.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Assuming you do in fact want to go to Lunar Isle instead of staying here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing captain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (ahem)

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have heard some terrible, scurrilous rumours about your wanton and depraved behaviour towards the Moon Clan when on shore leave.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: What? What ARE you talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come now, we all know what you were up to...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your behaviour was so bad that the Moon Clan have brought a terrible curse down upon the ship, trapping it on this gods-forsaken island forever!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Hmmm. Well, I'm sure the crew have more sense than to listen to the insane babble of a stowaway, especially not one whose rantings are designed to cover his own jinxed

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you deny it then?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Deny what? You come in here, spouting some random bilge at me, with a flurry of accusations, and expect anyone to take you seriously?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.. erm... Well done. You've passed the test.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Test?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... the, uh, loyalty test. The Captain wanted to make sure his crew were all well behaved on their shore leave, so I came up with this little test to make sure you were.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And congratulations! You passed!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: A stowaway, a jinx, AND a liar.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I must say I am extremely glad you are only here temporarily, I would hate for any of my crew to have any sort of dealing with you.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Please make sure you close the door on your way out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, 'bye then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: AH-HA! I KNOW it was YOU!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, what is it now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it seems that someone must have offended the Moon Clan, so that they put a jinx on the ship!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And from what I've seen you're the obvious culprit!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: No, I don't think so.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: This seems once again an effort on your behalf to throw the trail away from your own jinxed self bringing us misfortune.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... You didn't offend any Moon Clan while you were on their island?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you REALLY sure?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I am the very model of self-restraint. As you should have realised by now, as I haven't yet given the order to have you put to death. Yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hum. Are you sure?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: REALLY sure?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yes. I suggest you check elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. But I've got my eye on you Mr Navigator!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yes, and I certainly have my eye on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know that visit to Lunar Isle you lot have just made...?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Oh yeah! It was great! They're kind of spooky, but hey, it was a great do they laid on for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't see anyone, you know, acting inappropriately or anything did you?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... Anyone doing anything that would offend them?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Nope, can't say as I did...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On Lunar Isle...

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't... You know. Do anything offensive towards the Moon Clan did you?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Okay, it was worth asking anyway.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: 'Bye then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seeya.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard you were a little bit merry on the old Lunar Isle expedition...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Possibly so merry that you did something to offend the Moon Clan and unleash a horrible curse upon us all...

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Heavens, no! Just because I'm a pirate doesn't mean I don't treat all cultures no matter how different with the utmost respect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it wasn't you?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Nope. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quick question: Do you know anyone who offended the Moon Clan on your recent visit?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Quick answer: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dude! Duuuuuuude! Dude!

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: I'm sorry, do I know you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dude! How about them Moon Clan, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, we offended them so hard on that shore leave, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You, uh, didn't do anything to offend the Moon Clan on shore leave, did you?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I look like an idiot now, don't I?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: You do, yes. Very much so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It would probably be best if I just left now, instead of carrying on talking, wouldn't it?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: It would, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and do that then...

sections.title: Talking to 'Beefy' Burns while in search of the jinx

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Sorry, the galley's closed fer restockings! Yer'll hafta come back later if'n ye be wanting food!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm not after food, I'm after information...

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Wussat? Some kind of alkyhol? If'n it ain't grog, I don't be stocking it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Can I just ask you a few questions?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye, that yer may, long'n as yer don't mind me carrying on with me restockings while we talk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's fine. I appreciate your time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... I have reason to believe that a jinx has befallen the ship due to someone offending the Moon Clan somehow... I was wondering if you knew anything about that.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: The Moon Clan yer say? Ah, well I dinna know much about them folks, when everyone else went to that there big old feast I stayed aboard the ship...

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: I gots ter be honest, I never trust any food I ain't prepared meself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... So the whole crew except yourself were at a big feast on Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was there drink involved at this feast?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye, ter be sure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So a bunch of drunken pirates feasting on Lunar Isle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I have my suspect for when the Moon Clan were offended.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the help there, Beefy!

sections.title: Talking to the crew about the feast

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Hi. Did you find the jinx yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Funny you should ask, I have a few questions for you about that.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Oh no... Look, I'm SO SORRY, I SWEAR it wasn't my fault!
