lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Erm, sorry, nothing, what did you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well, it was about that feast that you all went to on Lunar Isle.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Oh, the feast, right, yeah, I was definitely there at that, absolutely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... Did you notice anyone not there? Or anyone acting very rudely to the Moon Clan? Anything like that?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Um, no, no, everyone was there, yup.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing at all?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Nope, we had a meal and then, um, some other stuff happened...

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Um... And then... Er, we all went to bed for an early night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? That doesn't sound much like the big raucous feast I've been hearing about from other people...

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: No, it TOTALLY happened EXACTLY like that. I swear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Weird. Maybe I'm barking up the wrong tree...

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Yup, sounds like it, okay, well 'bye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That big feast in Lunar Isle...

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Yes? What about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was everyone there?

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Except for the Captain, I think so, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And did you see anyone misbehaving particularly badly?

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: No, I don't think I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Hi. How's the search for the jinx going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not very well to be honest...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've worked out that whatever happened to cause the Moon Clan offence and get this ship cursed somehow must have happened at that big feast you all went to, but I'm still no wiser as to what actually

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: happened...

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Well, if you think it will help I can tell you everything I remember about the feast...?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually yeah, that might help give me some clue as to what happened. Please, go ahead.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Well now, let's see...

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: I recall that we all headed onto the island as we were due shore leave, and the Captain had arranged a big feast with the Moon Clan so that we could kick back and relax.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: I think we were all there, but we had a table to ourselves where they just brought us food and wine all evening.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: I remember Beefy the cook was being a bit of a killjoy and had stayed on board the ship, and the Captain was having some fancy meal with the Moon Clan toffs, but I'm pretty sure the rest of us were all at the meal...

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Anyway, spirits got a little high later on after we'd finished eating, and I remember Tommy Two-times got out his old fiddle, and we sang and danced all through the night until daybreak when we made our way back

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: aboard the ship, and set sail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When you say there were high spirits... You don't think perhaps you accidentally offended the Moon Clan somehow do you?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: No, not at all! A couple of charming Moon Clan members came over and danced along with us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thanks for the help, but I don't think I'm any closer to solving this whole jinx mystery...

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Well, I wish you luck, because it is very unnerving to think that I'm sailing on a cursed ship!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. I don't suppose you saw anyone acting suspiciously at that big feast on Lunar Isle did you?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was worth asking anyway, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. That big feast you went to on Lunar Isle...

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Sorry, I don't remember much about it, I'd had a fair bit to drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? Do you think maybe while drunk you did something to offend them?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: I don't see how, not with my condition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Condition?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Yes, it's a very rare medical condition.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: When I'm drunk I become completely silent and immobile, and just sit quietly by myself until I have sobered up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, this is a genuine medical condition is it?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Oh, absolutely. I'm in the medical books and everything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Okay then, I guess you're off the hook.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. You know that big feast you went to on Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: I do indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were there all night with the rest of the crew?

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: I was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Didn't see anyone leave early?

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Not that I recall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Didn't do anything to offend the Moon Clan, or see anyone else doing anything like that?

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Nope, can't say as I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Captain!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Mmmmm? You have information on what has happened to my ship for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not as such, but I think I am onto a promising lead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently you all went to a big feast on Lunar Isle held by the Moon Clan when last you visited?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: That is correct, yes. I was most pleasantly surprised by their hospitality.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I don't want to go flinging out unfounded accusations or anything, but I have a strong suspicion that somebody at that feast did something that resulted in the Moon Clan getting offended and causing this jinx

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: to be placed on the ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In fact, it might not even be the ship that's been cursed, it could just be this person, or even some item they're carrying.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Hmmm. You know, that's some good thinking. You're right, I suspect something like that did indeed happen.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Do you have any idea who this person may have been?

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Brakawk* Who's a jinx? Who's a jinx?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm afraid not. But with your permission, I will ask around the crew and see if anybody knows anything.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: You obviously have my full permission, I have no desire for a jinx to be aboard my vessel, and if this has been the result of one of my crews thoughtless actions, then I will of course have to reprimand them severely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I thought you might say that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So let's begin with you: Where were you at the night of the feast, and what exactly were you doing?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: WHAT? You suspect ME of having been jinxed???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not really, but it pays to be methodical in something like this.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Ah, yes, I understand. Well now...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: As captain, I was first envoy to the Moon Clan and took seat with their dignitaries and the higher ranking members of their tribe, the Oneiromancer, Baba Yaga and so forth.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I do recall we had a most intriguing conversation about the history of their people, and their manufacturing of rune-stones for trade across the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the rest of your crew weren't with you?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: No, they were seated at a different table towards the back of the hall. I felt that they deserved a little recreation and relaxation time without their captain hanging around.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: They're a good crew and have served me well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't actually know which of the crew were present at this feast? For all you know somebody might have slipped away from the feast and, oh I don't know, stolen some

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: artefact of theirs, or broken into somewhere they weren't supposed to go?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Well, of course that is all possible, but I wouldn't think any of my crew would be so rude as to break the gracious hospitality we had been offered!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: We're pirates after all, not barbarians!

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: *Squawk* Not barbarians! Not barbarians! *Squawk*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, thank you for your time Captain, I'll see if anybody else noticed someone slipping away during the feast, or noticed someone behaving particularly badly towards any of the Moon Clan.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Please keep me informed of your discoveries. A jinx is an evil thing, and if one of my crew has brought it upon the Lady Zay, then I want to know about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahoy there! I have a few questions for you.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Hmmmm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's about that feast that you all went to on Lunar Isle...

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: What of it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You had a good time at it, right?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Well of course. Even the first officer has to have a little relaxation every so often.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you didn't get drunk and start a fight with anyone, or insult your hosts the Moon Clan?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Well, of course not. Even in my spare time I have to show a little decorum.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Set an example to the men and all that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you definitely didn't insult any of the Moon Clan at the feast?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Absolutely not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, ok. You didn't see anyone else getting too rowdy did you?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: No I didn't, and let me assure you that if I HAD they would have been most severely reprimanded by myself if not by the Captain.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I won't tolerate that kind of behaviour amongst my crew, even if they are in their free time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some questions for you about that big feast you all went to on Lunar Isle.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh no, not you again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: AH-HA! IS that an admission of guilt?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: How is that an admission of guilt???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you didn't deny anything.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: What's to deny??? You haven't asked me anything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A minor technicality.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: (sigh) Please... Please just ask me your questions then leave me alone...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well okay then. So tell me Mr Navigator, where were you exactly at this feast on Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Er... at the feast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, really?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yeees....

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you claim to have been at this feast the whole time that you were at this feast?

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Um... yes, how could I not have been at the feast the whole time that I was at the feast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm asking the questions here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you were at the feast. So you say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I put it to you Mr Navigator that once you had arrived at the feast, when it grew quieter and nobody noticed, you left and decided to steal something from the Moon Clan, and be very rude to some other

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: members of the Moon Clan on the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then you... I don't know. Killed their puppies or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then after you had done all that, you returned to the feast, before anyone noticed that you had left, and then made sure your alibi was complete, and that nobody would discover your rude, thieving, puppy murdering

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ways.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Look, I've had just about enough of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: AH-HA! I notice you don't deny it!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: ' I'm not even going to dignify that with a response, you have come in here with a string of falsehoods against me, and apparently have some vendetta against me for some reason unknown to me, when I think it's pretty

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: clear that if anybody is a jinx on this ship it is you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: AM NOT!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: ARE TOO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, this is getting us nowhere, I promise you if you confess I will make the Captain go easy on you!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Confess to WHAT exactly??? I have done nothing wrong!

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I went to a feast on Lunar Isle, I stayed with the rest of the crew the whole night, I didn't see anyone offending them, and I didn't do anything to offend them myself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, that's all I wanted to know. See you around.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: You again? You still looking for the jinx then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I just have a few quick questions for you...

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Ask away. I have nothing to hide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I just wanted to ask you about that big feast on Lunar Isle you went to...

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Ask away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see anyone slip away during it? Or see anyone acting extremely badly? Or maybe you saw someone being rude to the Moon Clan somehow?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: I'm sorry, I didn't see much beyond the food on my plate and the grog in my tankard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll have to continue checking around.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Well, good luck with that. I'm very uneasy knowing that there's a jinx on board.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Hey, how you doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well thanks, I just have a few questions I'd like to ask you about that big feast at Lunar Isle...

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Ah yes, those were the days!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good, was it?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Oh, like you wouldn't believe... The food... The wine... The women... The songs...

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: It was everything any honest pirate could ever hope for!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you were all there?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Hmmm... I think so...

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Except the Captain obviously.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you didn't see anyone behaving particularly badly? Or being rude to any of the Moon Clan or anything?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Can't say as I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there.

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if I could ask you a few questions about the big feast you went to on Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Oh man, that feast totally RAWKED!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you got a little bit merry at it?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Oh man, like you WOULDN'T BELIEVE!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't get... TOO overboard with your fun did you?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: No way man, I know when enough's enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't see anyone else going... a little bit too far with their fun either?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: No, I have to say everybody was surprisingly well behaved considering it was our first shore leave in quite a few months!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That big feast on Lunar Isle... You were there right? The whole evening?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Yup.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Didn't see anyone being rude to the Moon Clan? Weren't being rude yourself?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Didn't see anyone sneak away in the middle of the feast or anything?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then, that's all I wanted to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you know that big feast they held for us on Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: Us? What 'us'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, you. And the rest of the crew.

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: Yes, I remember it well. It was a surprisingly restrained evening on all of our parts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So no crazy offensive behaviours from anyone.

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: No, not at all. Everybody was surprisingly well behaved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... And the whole crew was there the whole time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't see anyone sneaking off mid-feast or anything?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: No, sorry, I don't believe I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there Beefy!

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Ah, it be you agin, what can i do yer for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About this big feast that all the pirates went to on Lunar Isle...

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: All the pirates cept'n me yer mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, this big feast that everybody went to except for you.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: What did yer want ter know abaht it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, just what information you could tell me about it. Who was there, what food was served, what entertainment was laid on if any, where it was held.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, the little details. I'm hoping I can catch someone out in a lie.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Yer wants me ter be telling yers all about a feast I wasnae even at?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... yes. If you could.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: I thinks yer need to be laying off of the old alkyhol, mate.

sections.title: Talking to 'Lecherous' Lee about the feast

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a couple of questions to ask you about the big feast on Lunar Isle you went to last time you went there.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Aaaahhhh yes.... I was quite taken with their hospitality!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see anyone being rude to the Moon Clan at all?

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Well, we were all in high spirits, but I certainly didn't see anything like that going on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I don't suppose you saw anyone slip away in the middle of the festival or anything?

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Other than the First Mate, no I didn't see anyone slip away from the feast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay tha- Wait, you say you saw the First Mate slip away in the middle of the feast?

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: That I did. I don't think he saw me see him leave, but it was definitely him.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: I watched him head towards our ship, and he came back, oh, I don't know, twenty minutes later or so.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: I doubt anyone else saw it, he just slipped away in the midst of the festivities.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, you've been very helpful.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: No problem.

sections.title: Talking to the crew after suspecting the first mate

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So now I think that the First Mate may have something to do with the jinx that has befallen this ship.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: What? Why? Because I said I saw him slip away in the middle of the feast?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm.. Yes?

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: I said I saw him slip away, but I don't believe he had anything to do with whatever angered the Moon Clan, if that's the lead you're chasing up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think he was doing then?

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: I have no idea. Maybe you should ask him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some good news!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: After much to-ing and fro-ing, I've discovered that not everybody who was supposed to be at the feast actually was!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: W-what???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it seems as though SOMEBODY slipped off in the middle of the feast, thinking nobody would see them do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unfortunately for them, somebody DID see them leave, and I'm pretty sure whatever they were up to caused this jinx.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I'M SO SORRY! I SWEAR I DIDN'T MEAN TO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, did you see the First Mate leave as well?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: W-what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not what you were talking about?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Um, no, no it really was, yup, so somebody saw the first mate leave and not, oh, say, saw me, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You left the feast?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Ahahahahaha no, no, not at all, definitely not, nope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So anyway, I figure I'll have this whole jinx thing straightened out after a quick chat with the First Mate.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: So it was all his fault? What a relief, uh, tragedy that has befallen us that is entirely all his fault and not mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The First Mate..

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: What about him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm thinking he might be responsible for the jinx on the ship.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Why are you thinking that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you know. It's fifty percent baseless gossip and fifty percent unfounded speculation.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: ...I see. I don't for a second agree with you, but I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So tell me... What do you know about the First Mate?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Davey-boy? What do you want to know about HIM for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it turns out that he was the only member of the crew other than the Bosun and the Captain who did not attend the feast on Lunar Isle.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: No, he was there, I distinctly remember him telling me to slow down on the drinks in case I made a fool of myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, but I have reason to believe that sometime during the meal he slipped away and did something or other.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can only assume that he was up to no good when he slipped away, and whatever he did caused the ship to be cursed...

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: We're talking about the same Davey-boy here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so... The First Mate, yeah.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: I don't believe it of him. Honestly, I can't say I like the man, but he has integrity.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: He would never knowingly do anything that might hurt this ship or her crew in any way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then perhaps unknowingly...?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Again, I doubt it. He is a very cautious man, often thinking ahead of what consequences might arise from any given action.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: The things that make him a very boring human being are what makes him a superb first mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you refuse to believe that he had anything at all to do with the jinx that's been placed somewhere on this ship?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: I suggest you speak to him about this, because I cannot for one second believe he has had anything to do with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, you've been very helpful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... The First Mate. What can you tell me about him?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Not a lot. He's a bit of a slave driver.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think he might have been responsible for the jinx that's been placed upon the ship?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Him? Naaaaaaaah...

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: He's way too straight-laced to have been up to anything dodgy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know that big feast you went to? I have reason to believe that the First Mate snuck away in the middle of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am forced to conclude he did so in order to get up to shenanigans.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Shenanigans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed!

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: I'm not exactly sure what you mean by shenanigans, but the First Mate is an honest, hard-working man.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: HE would never do anything to harm the ship or its crew.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmmm. Maybe I'd better go speak to him about it.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: I think you'd better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you remember seeing the first mate at the feast?

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Only for part of it. I was pretty drunk though. I'm not sure I remember anything but the start of the evening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Captain! I think I've made a breakthrough!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Oh? Please, do tell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it seems that while the rest of the crew were at the feast, the First Mate snuck away by himself and was up to shenanigans!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Davey-boy?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Rubbish. I don't believe it. You must be mistaken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I have a witness...

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I don't care. Not him. No way. I suggest you go and speak to him about this, whatever he did he would have had a very good reason for,

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: and would certainly never put this ship or her crew in any sort of risk.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: He's just not that sort of a person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and see what he has to say for himself then.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Oh, what now???

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just thought you might like to know, that I have reluctantly decided that the problems with the ship may possibly in some small degree perhaps actually not be entirely down to your incompetence after all.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: That's your way of apologising for falsely accusing me of all that stuff earlier is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, because an apology would imply that I have done something wrong, and I still think that your rubbishness at, er, navigatorness, is a contributing factor towards the ships problems.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And when I say contributing factor I mean it's still mostly your fault.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Why, you insufferable...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And let me assure you, if I find out that you were in cahoots with the First Mate to bring this jinx upon us, then I will not hesitate to bring it to the Captains attention.

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Wait, you mean you're saying you think the First Mate has done this???

lines.speaker: 'Birds-Eye' Jack
lines.text: No. No way. You're even more off base with your crazy assumptions than ever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, that big feast you went to...

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: I already told you, I didn't see anyone acting out of order at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's kind of what I wanted to ask you about...

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I understand the First Mate snuck out in the middle of the feast...

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Did he? I can't say as I remember...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you didn't see him at the feast, and you didn't notice that he wasn't there?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Sorry. You know how it is at a party!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, you know that big feast you all went to?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Well, we weren't all there. The Captain was at the high table, and the Bosun didn't trust their food and stayed on ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would it surprise you to hear that the First Mate was also not there?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: It would indeed, because I saw him there with my own eyes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it on very good authority that he slipped out in the middle of the feast for about twenty minutes or so.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Really? Well, I guess he could have, I wasn't watching him all night...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas what he might have been up to?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: None at all I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Hello to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... I heard a rumour...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That maybe this ship going in a circle business is down to the First Mate...

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Hmmmm? What makes you say that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it seems he slipped away unnoticed at that big feast you all went to.

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Really? Well, he must have had some official business to do.

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Trust me, that guy? He wouldn't do anything to harm the ship or it's crew in any way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Honestly. I suggest you go and speak to him, I'll bet you anything he has a good explanation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and do that then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I heard that the First Mate wasn't at that big feast!

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Really. And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: No, that's what I was asking you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll ask the questions here!

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Well what did you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, um, can't remember now...

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Maybe you'd better go think about it then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, I'm getting the feeling that this jinx upon the ship might be the First Mate's fault.

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: Why would you think that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, apparently he slipped away in the middle of the feast, so my guess is he was up to something when he did that.

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: The First Mate? No, that doesn't sound like him at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I dunno, my evidence against him is pretty good...

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: No, it definitely wouldn't be him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, you were onboard the ship the night of that big feast on Lunar Isle weren't you?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye, ter be sure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you happen to notice, oh, say, the First Mate come aboard while everybody else was at the feast?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Ter be honest, I don't remember much of what happened terday, let alone stuff that happened awhiles back

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... okay then.

sections.title: Talking to First mate 'Davey-boy' after suspecting him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... Finally we get to the truth... MISTER Davey-boy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: IF that's even your REAL name.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Hmmm?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: You're finally confessing to being a stowaway and a jinx?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? NO

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, Davey, this is about YOU!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is about your LIES and your BAD behaviour!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Oh, what on Gielinor are you on about now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know your SECRET Mr first mate! I know that you are a PHONY!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know that you were NOT where you claimed to be on the night in question!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Night in question? Phony? Secret?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: What ARE you blathering about now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, come come, Davey-boy! It is too late to weasel your way out of this one!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I have absolutely no idea what on earth you are talking about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you know... your ALIBI?!?!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Alibi?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: YOU know. How you told me that you were at that feast on Lunar Isle ALL night?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, let me tell YOU that you are a LIAR!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have an eyewitness that SAW you leave the feast, and go off and do who-knows-what for a good TWENTY MINUTES!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it is time to come clean, Davey-boy. I think we should hear the truth.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: What? Well of COURSE I was at that feast all ni...

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: ...Hmmm. You know, now that you come to mention it, you are correct, I did leave the feast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: AH-HA! I KNEW IT!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Oh, do calm down, it was nothing particularly interesting.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I merely had to inspect the rigging was secure before the ship was docked securely for the night.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: It's one of those things you have to do, in case a storm blows up while we're asleep and yanks the ship off to sea unattended.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why didn't you tell me this before?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Well, clearly it slipped my mind.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: It's not one of my usual responsibilities, so obviously I didn't think much of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, what do you mean it's not one of your usual responsibilities?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: It's the cabin boys' job to ensure that the rigging and the sails have been safely secured before disembarking the ship.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Pirates Regulations #445-328.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why didn't he?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I have no idea. I couldn't find him at the feast, so I left to secure them myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, so he wasn't even at the feast?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Not that I could see, no. And I do recall I spent some good time looking for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the cabin boy wasn't at the feast at all? WHY DIDN'T YOU TELL ME EARLIER?!?!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: I suppose it slipped my mind. Surely it can't be of any importance, a runt like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's only the single most important thing you've said to me since I've met you!

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: If you say so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I guess this explains why the Cabin Boy's been acting so suspiciously... I'd better go and have a word with him!

sections.title: Talking to the crew after suspecting the Cabin boy

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Captain?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: The last time I checked, yes, I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I meant, 'Captain? Can I have a word with you?'

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: You just did didn't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (sigh) Let's start again...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Captain, I need to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Speak away. How goes the search for the jinx?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I may have found who was responsible for upsetting the Moon Clan.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Hmmm? Who would that be then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm pretty sure it was the Cabin Boy.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: As you have already accused two of my crew unfairly, may I ask what leads you to this conclusion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well he seems to be the only member of the crew who was absent without a good reason to be so.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I see. And have you spoken to him about this?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: It is very possible he has a valid reason to have not been present.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: As I understand it Pirate Regulation number 445-328 required him to have been checking the sails.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but the First Mate was doing that instead of him.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: That's very strange. So what does he have to say for himself... Have you asked him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... Not yet, no.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Then I suggest you do so. Immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm pretty sure now that it was the Cabin Boy that has caused all this.

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I wish you would make your mind up, or are you just going to go around accusing everyone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if I did accuse everyone, eventually I would be right.

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Your powers of investigation are impressive, for sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey! Where'd the Cabin Boy go? I need to speak to him!

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Why... That's odd, he was here a minute ago!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Know where he might have headed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm pretty sure that the Cabin Boy is responsible for the jinx on this ship.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Aye? Well yer best be speakin' ter him 'bout that then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm pretty sure that the Cabin Boy is responsible for the jinx on this ship.

lines.speaker: [Crewmember]
lines.text: Really? Then you had best speak to him about it.

sections.title: Talking to the Cabin boy after suspecting him

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was you!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, you left in the middle of the feast, what did you do? You're a spy for the Moon Clan aren't you.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Admit it!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go get the captain, a plank and a hungry shark!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Ok, OK! I confess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! 1-nil to the [player name]!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: There's no need to brag! Yes, I left the feast. I was lured by a girl from the Moon Clan, she promised me control of the ship if I did this simple task for her. I couldn't resist. Have you any idea how hard it is being

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: a boy on a ship full of pirates?! I had no idea it would turn out how it has.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did she ask you to do?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: She showed me some symbols, she then asked me to draw five of them throughout the ship. Some kind of magic jinx. Please don't tell the Captain! He'll kill me! If I ever get to that island again, I'm going to see what

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: she has to say for herself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I dunno. Some people! I will see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: You'll need a special kind of lantern to see them, there are five symbols in total I think. I vaguely remember what I put them on. One was up on a wall.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: One was on a container. One was on a box of some sort. One was on a support. The last one is hazy, all I remember is that it was big and metal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You best not be lying!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I'm not! Honest. Look, I have a lantern and lens here, put them together then light it with a tinderbox. Then you can use it to search the ship for the symbols.

sections.title: Talking to the crew after learning about the symbols

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrr!

lines.speaker: ['Lecherous' Lee/'Sticky' Sanders]
lines.text: Arrrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrrrrrrrr!

lines.speaker: ['Lecherous' Lee/'Sticky' Sanders]
lines.text: Arrrrrrrrrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: AAARRRRRRRRR!!!!!

lines.speaker: ['Lecherous' Lee/'Sticky' Sanders]
lines.text: AAAAARRRRRR!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, i'm bored of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really can't remember where you drew those symbols?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I vaguely remember what I put them on. One was up on a wall. One was on a container. One was on a box of some sort.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: One was on a support. The last one is hazy, all I remember is that it was big and metal. Use the lantern and lens together and light with a

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: tinderbox. Then you can use it to search the ship for the symbols.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You best not be lying!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really can't remember where you drew those symbols?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I vaguely remember what I put them on. One was up on a wall. One was on a container. One was on a box of some sort.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: One was on a support. The last one is hazy, all I remember is that it was big and metal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You best not be lying!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I'm not! Honest. Look, use the lantern and lens together and light it with a tinderbox. Then you can use it to search the ship for the symbols.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oi! I need another lens, boy.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: But you don't have space.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good point.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: By all means, here you go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oi! I need another lantern, boy.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: But you don't have space.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good point.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: By all means, here you go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it turns out it was the Cabin Boy. I just need to find some symbols he's written now.

lines.speaker: ['Beefy' Burns/'Eagle-eye' Shultz]
lines.text: I knew he was up to no good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm on the search for symbols. Seen any?

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Nope, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yo ho yo ho! A pirates life for me!

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: How do ye know that sacred song of the pirates?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought it was pretty much common knowledge.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: It is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Arrrrr!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for symbols. Have you seen...

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: You do know both of these eyes are made of glass don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ooops. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Ah, you again. So what did the Cabin Boy have to say for himself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems he has been drawing symbols over various surfaces in the ship. Don't be too hard on him though.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Don't worry, I was a young'un once. I remember when I used to have all my fingers and toes, and when my right eye wasn't made of glass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, we all remember those days. Sort of...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it turns out it was the cabin boy. I just need to find some symbols he's written now.

lines.speaker: [First mate 'Davey-boy'/'Picarron' Pete]
lines.text: I knew he was up to no good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you've seen any magic symbols around?

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: None at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, that's no surprise as you need a special lantern to see them.

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Best you get a move on then. My reputation as a navigator is at stake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you've seen any symbols around have you?

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Written in chalk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it!

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Strange magical symbols?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: That look like they were drawn by a member of the moon clan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly!

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's things?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: What do you mean 'how's things'? Here we are going in circles waiting for you to sort out this jinx, and you ask 'how's things'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just trying to be polite.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: Don't bother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seen any symbols around here.

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: None at all - you thinking of making some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no, i'm trying to remove them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can find any magic symbols?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: No sorry.

sections.title: Using the emerald lantern on the ship's scenery
If the scenery does not have a symbol
If the scenery has a symbol

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah-ha! I've found one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One down, four more to go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Two down, three more to go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Three down, only two more to go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Four down, only one more to go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's it, I've removed all the symbols, and hopefully the jinx.

sections.title: Talking to the crew after removing the jinx

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found all the symbols.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Best you go talk to the captain then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well lucky for you I found all the symbols.

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Phew. Good work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrr!

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Arrrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrrrrrrrr!

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Arrrrrrrrrr!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: AAARRRRRRRRR!!!!!

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: AAAAARRRRRR!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, i'm bored of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have discovered all the symbols!

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: What, you want a medal or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think i've removed the jinx now.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Well done, we'll make a pirate out of you yet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've removed all the symbols.

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Best you get us on our way then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The jinx should be lifted now. You're free to navigate us to the island!

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: That kind of order can only come from the Captain, I'm afraid. Go speak with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Can't you see i'm busy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... no to be honest you seem pretty care-free at the moment.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Well don't be deceived by looks! I'm busy practicing looking fierce.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: It's very important to strike fear into the enemy just by looks. It's rule 1 of being a pirate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whatever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it like being a pirate?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: That's a strange question. I'ts not too bad I suppose. Sure, the pay is good, but the hours are long and we don't get many holidays.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you not have a union to help you?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: A pirates union? No, they would stab us in the back at the first opportunity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a shame.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The jinx has been removed!

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: What jinx?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nevermind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is your favourite tipple?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: Drink? Big beards Frothy Rum headbanger!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahhh, a true classic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The jinx on the ship should have been removed now.

lines.speaker: [Crewmember]
lines.text: Well done!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Bentley after removing the jinx

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The jinx should be gone now!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Really? Good work, landlubber!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No worries. It was all down to the cabin boy, it seems.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Pesky kid. Ah, but I remember when I was a cabin boy. Many years ago, I started off as...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Spare me the life story, can we go now?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: No need to be rude.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Very well. It's off we go!

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Here we are! Lunar Isle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Cap'n.

sections.title: Talking to the crew after sailing to Lunar Isle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's time to go exploring around this island.

lines.speaker: 'Lecherous' Lee
lines.text: Good luck, tis a wierd place for sure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we've made it to the island. What are you waiting for - I thought you were going to go see that girl?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: I will, I will. I'm just psyching myself up first!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's probably for the best. You'll need to be psyched up to confront her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm off to the island now.

lines.speaker: 'Sticky' Sanders
lines.text: Fare well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do for fun on this ship? You know, when you're not doing pirate stuff.

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: All sorts! Hide and seek, pin the patch on the pirate, walk the plank!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What a life! Wait a minute. 'Walk the plank'? Surely that's a bit dangerous?

lines.speaker: 'Eagle-eye' Shultz
lines.text: Well of course, but where's the fun without a few deaths?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll stick to Runelink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At last - Lunar Isle!

lines.speaker: Jack Sails
lines.text: Woop de do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel a bit sick.

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: Sea sick?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it's the smell. Can't you smell it?

lines.speaker: Murky Pat
lines.text: I can just smell that beautiful smell of fish guts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We have arrived at the island.

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Really? You don't think a pirate would know the difference of being out at sea and being at a dock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't say anything right with you Pirates.

lines.speaker: Jenny Blade
lines.text: Well get the hint then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does it take to become first mate on a ship?

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: Good question. We have a diplomatic consession at the turn of the financial year. Said pirate is chosen should the existing mate be absent without leave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had no idea. I always figured it was all about popularity.

lines.speaker: First mate 'Davey-boy'
lines.text: It is. I'm just pulling your leg.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel a little sea-sick from this travelling.

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Well there's a good remedy for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is?

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: Swim.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh HA HA. Thanks for the help!

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: My pleasure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was being sarcastic.

lines.speaker: 'Bird's-Eye' Jack
lines.text: I know. So was I.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking forward to looking around the island.

lines.speaker: Tommy 2-times
lines.text: Arrr, tis a bootiful place to be sure. Watch your step though. The Moon Clan don't suffer outsiders easily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be careful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you miss your family, y'know, being a pirate out as sea?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: You assume I have a family?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, you don't?

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: I do. 16 kids, 12 nephews and 18 nieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! I pity your wife.

lines.speaker: Bedread the bold
lines.text: I wouldn't, she never cooks my dinner when I get home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like we have arrived at the island.

lines.speaker: 'Picarron' Pete
lines.text: Aye, let the feasting begin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should I remember anything when visiting this island? Can you give me any advice?

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Yeah, don't scratch your nose with an axe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does that have to do with the island.

lines.speaker: 'Beedy-eye' Jones
lines.text: Well if you don't want my advice...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you ever get sea-sick?

lines.speaker: 'Betty' B.Boppin
lines.text: Nah. I get land sick though, i'm always glad to return to the waves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How bizarre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you cooking?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: My speciality! What else could I be cooking?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, and your speciality is...?

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Boiled shark guts with a hint of rosemary and a dash of squid ink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll stick to making my own food.

lines.speaker: 'Beefy' Burns
lines.text: Your loss!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Bentley on Lunar Isle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: And you're wanting what now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you take me back to Rellekka please?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: I'll take you as far as Pirates' Cove. You'll have to find the rest of the way back yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we're here?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Yep. You're free to explore the island. Be careful though, the Moon Clan are very powerful, it wouldn't be wise to wrong them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll keep that seal of passage close.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Bentley on Pirates' Cove after visiting Lunar Isle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we head to Lunar Isle?

lines.speaker: Captain Bentley
lines.text: Sure matey!

sections.title: Talking to the Cabin boy on Lunar Isle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you've plucked up the courage to come and confront that girl!

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: That I did, that I did!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: She turned out to be really nice! She's joining us to become a pirate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? And you're not sceptical about this? You know, after what she did last time?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: She---has---no---spell---on---me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? Why are you talking like that?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like someone else was talking for you. Are you sure she hasn't put a spell on you again? Did she wave a watch in front of your face? You know, tell you to look deep into her eyes?

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: She---has---no---spell---on---me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you've been hypnotised. I wonder what happens if I click my fingers?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *click*

lines.speaker: Cabin boy
lines.text: *Cluck* *cluck* *bwaarrk*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear. Oh well, I'm sure you'll learn one day.

sections.title: Talking to Baba Yaga after after arriving to Lunar Isle

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah! A visitor from a distant land! How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a very interesting house you have here. Does he have a name?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Why of course. It's Berty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Berty? Berty the Chicken leg house?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: May I ask why?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: It just has that certain ring to it, don't you think? Beeerteeee!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're ins...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Insane? Very.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm good thanks, bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take me to your leader.

lines.speaker: Meteora
lines.text: Wot?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't that what you're supposed to say when you meet a new race of people?

lines.speaker: Meteora
lines.text: Well mate, we don't av no leader, so I ain't gonna take ya to um. We auw av eqwuw right's un stuff, yeah? But if ya wont an intro go live it up wiv da Onerimancer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK. Is he rich and powerful?

lines.speaker: Meteora
lines.text: No man, SHE, is dead-wise yeah? She is responsible for initiation wituals an stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. Thanks. And where is she?

lines.speaker: Meteora
lines.text: She angs out int souf east of ouw island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okey dokey...yeah.

lines.speaker: Meteora
lines.text: Huh?

sections.title: Trying to craft a rune at the Astral altar
Trying to pray at the Astral altar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Why hello, how are you today?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Fine. Yes I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? How'd you...

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: By reading your mind. But that's not important. We have some important things to discuss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We do? This is going to take some time getting used to.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Of course! I can see that you come bearing great promise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great promise?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Please. Just tell me why you have come to our island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm seeking some kind of settlement between your clan and the Fremenniks. I understand there has been a dispute for a great many years and being a member of the Fremennik clan I wanted to come as a

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: representative to see if something couldn't be sorted out.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: A very honourable objective, but how do you suppose this will be possible?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I was hoping you may be able to help me out with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more of why you don't feel your people get on with the Fremenniks?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: We are very in tune with magic as you have no doubt observed, but we have yet to meet one Fremennik with the perspective that this is of any value. They are always picking at us to reveal our secrets, but why

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: should we when they view us so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you'd be surprised at how flexible they could be. I think they just feel like you put yourselves on a higher pedestal to look down at them. They don't like that and can't see what is so special about you and

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: your ways.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hmmm. Well you are one of them. Perhaps if you can understand our ways, that will at least be a start. How does that sound to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I am always eager to learn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of thing had you in mind?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Well, we have a ritual called 'The View of Self Dream'. It is a ritual that we all have to go through. You see the path to understanding magic is through understanding ourselves. It is a power innate to every

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: living creature, but it takes a great understanding of self to tap into. One of the best ways to understand ourselves is through our dreams. These are a demonstration of our subconscious, our subconscious

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: being raw and true. But it takes many years to get into a situation where you are ready to attempt it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not so sure I want to wait years to solve this... Is there any way I can just attempt this ritual now?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: I don't mind you trying, but I really doubt you'll achieve anything. There are also a fair few preparations you'll have to make first too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's OK. What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You are going to need to do a few things. You'll need a waking sleep potion, a Lunar staff, and ceremonial traveling clothes. Start with the potion, I suggest you have a word with Baba Yaga in her chicken house,

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: she will be able to help you with the making of the potion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Chicken house?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Yes, you can't miss it, it's walking around the north of the town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, what was I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Seek Baba Yaga, she is in her chicken house to the north of our island. She should be able to help you out with the potion of waking sleep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah! A visitor from a distant land! How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Oneiromancer told me you may be able to help...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: With a potion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I will ever get used to people reading my mind. Yes, a potion of Waking Sleep? I need it to go the the...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Dreamland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know that's really quite rude!

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Sorry. Carry on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was hoping you could tell me how to make one?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Are you sure you're ready to take on the dream land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no harm in trying. Right...?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Well, some people have gone mad from visiting their dreams.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really know how to instil discomfort don't you!?! How do you make...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You'll need 1 guam leaf, 1 Marrentill leaf and a Suqah tooth ground using a pestle and mortar. Combine these in a vial with water and BAM! You've got your potion! You should be able to get the ingredients from the

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Suqah monsters.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You'll also need a special vial, I have one here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah! A visitor from a distant land! How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the potion you wanted me to make...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You'll need 1 guam leaf, 1 Marrentill leaf and a Suqah tooth ground using a pestle and mortar. Combine these in a vial with water and BAM! You've got your potion! You should be able to get the ingredients from the

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Suqah monsters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop pre-empting what I'm going to ask.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: I think you shall just have to get used to it on this island.

sections.title: Talking to the Oneiromancer without a waking sleep vial

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Have you got the potion to give me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Baba Yaga should be able to help you out with that.

sections.title: Talking to the Oneiromancer with a waking sleep vial

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Have you got the potion to give me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have indeed.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Well done, I'll take that for safe keeping.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what next?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Next we will sort you out with the staff before the clothes. You are going to have to get hold of a Dramen staff first. You will need to take it to the air, fire, water and earth altars to imbue it with power to convert it

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: into a Lunar staff. Bring that back to me and then we can get on with the next stage.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: And don't worry, you'll be able to do anything you can with the new enchanted version of the Dramen Staff as you could with its ordinary form! Including visiting the lost city!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Managed to get the Dramen staff enchanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet. What do I have to do?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You are going to have to get hold of a Dramen staff first, which you should be able to get from Entrana. Take it to the air, fire, water and earth altars, in that order, to embue it with power to convert it into a

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Lunar staff. Bring that back to me and then we can get on with the next stage.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: And don't worry, you'll be able to do anything you can with the new enchanted version of the Dramen Staff as you could with it's ordinary form! Including visiting the lost city!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: OK, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the Oneiromancer while holding the Lunar staff

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Managed to get the Dramen staff enchanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have indeed!

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Well done, pass it here.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Now we can get on with the next stage - your suit! There are 8 parts to this.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Which would you like to hear about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The helm.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: This helm needs to be made from an ore you can only get from the mine on this island. You'll see some stalagmites that you can mine from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The cape.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: There should be one of these floating around somewhere. Talk to some of the people in the village and I am sure one of them will be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The amulet.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You should go and speak with Meteora, she always seems to be wearing one on her head for some reason. She might give that to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The torso.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You'll find a monster called the Suqah that is indigenous to this island. You'll need one of their hides to make your torso, which you can tan only in this town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The gloves.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You'll find a monster called the Suqah that is indigenous to this island. You'll need one of their hides to make your gloves, which you can tan only in this town

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The boots.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You'll find a monster called the Suqah that is indigenous to this island. You'll need one of their hides to make your boots, which you can tan only in this town

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The trousers.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You'll find a monster called the Suqah that is indigenous to this island. You'll need one of their hides to make your trousers, which you can tan only in this town

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The ring.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You should probably go and speak to Selene, she will be able to help you as she always seems to have bits and pieces like that hanging around.

sections.title: Talking to the Oneiromancer again before getting the Lunar clothes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, could you refresh my memory: what am I supposed to be doing?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Well you need to be making your Lunar clothes.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Which would you like to hear about?

sections.title: Talking to Pauline Polaris to get the Lunar Cape

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm on the lookout for ceremonial clothing so I can visit my dreams. I don't suppose you could help me out?

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Ah, I know the ceremony very well. Maybe I can help. I do have a cape, but why would I want to give it to some random person off the streets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because you're a kind person and like helping people in need?

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Nice try. But I am always glad to help a person in need, if they can demonstrate some insight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm listening...

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: How about you try and guess my name!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bob?

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: How rude!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you do seem to have a moustache growing.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Get out of my sight!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pauline

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Ah, you'd think so, but no. That is my alias. I want you to tell me my REAL name! I have one first name, and a triple barrel surname!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no. Can't you give me some kind of clue?

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Let's see...

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: OK. Change a letter from the word Dane to get my first name. The first part of my surname rhymes with wood, the third part rhymes with spade and the second rhymes with magic.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Nope. Not even close.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Nope. Not even close.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: That's it! Well done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woo hoo! Not the most normal name is it?

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: It's not my fault my parents had an over active imagination.

lines.speaker: Player

sections.title: Talking to Pauline Polaris after guessing her alias name correctly

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I try guessing your name again to get the cape? And can I have the clues again?

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: I don't like to repeat myself, but...

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: OK. Change a letter from the word Dane to get my first name. The first part of my surname rhymes with wood, the third part rhymes with spade and the second rhymes with magic.

sections.title: Talking to Pauline Polaris after getting the cape

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Ah, a stranger to our island. What brings you here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I've actually come here on a diplomatic mission. I want to try and settle some of the disputes between the Fremenniks and your clan.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: The Fremenniks? Pah! They are just too ignorant and stubborn to listen to anything we have to say - how can we possibly associate with a race that won't listen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think they are very eager to listen, but they feel like you are keeping secrets from them.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Secrets?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, as in your magic ways. To be honest, I think it is fear of the unknown.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Well, when the day comes that a Fremennik can prove they have the patience and interest in learning our ways, then we will perhaps share our secrets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm hoping I might be able to achieve that.

lines.speaker: Pauline Polaris
lines.text: Good luck. It's far from simple.

sections.title: Talking to Selene to get the ring

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Greetings sweetie. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me a bit about your people?

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Ok. Like what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about the values of the Moon clan!

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Let me see... We value knowledge of self because it is this that gives us our strength! It is most important!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I know things about myself. I know I like hot chocolate!

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: I was meaning something a little deeper than that. We also like to see someone listen. You know how they say a wise man listens?

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Did you hear me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: .... I'm listening.

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Most wise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a ring.

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Well there are a great many rings darling, try to be more specific.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's to go with the ceremonial clothes I need for visiting the dream land.

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Ah, I see. I'm afraid to say I don't have one of those. But... I do know that my grandfather had such a ring, but he passed away a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: There is a chance he buried it though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Buried it? Why would he do a thing like that?

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: He was an archaeologist. Well, more of a treasure hunter. When I was little he always used to bury items around the island and give me clues and maps to find them. If you're lucky you

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: might find one of those rings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's worth a shot.

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: I remember those days fondly, hours of digging around, dodging boulders, pits of snakes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that ring...

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: Ah, yes.

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: The clue I have written down here says: From water's source you should follow, crossing the western-most bridge and travelling further south- westerly to a bloom of blue.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I hope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not going to talk to someone that calls me 'sweetie'!

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: No need to be rude dear!

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Selene
lines.text: The clue I have written down here says: From water's source you should follow, crossing the western-most bridge and travelling further south- westerly to a bloom of blue.

lines.speaker: Player
